question.text: In April 2020, WTI Oil Futures on Nymex turned negative primarily due to:

answer.text: Pandemic-induced lockdowns causing demand collapse and storage shortage
A sudden increase in global oil production by OPEC
Cyber attack on oil trading systems
US government intervention to reduce oil prices

explanation.text: **The Logic:** WTI Oil Futures turned negative in April 2020 due to the pandemic-induced lockdowns that collapsed demand and created a storage crisis — holders had to pay others to take delivery. **The Trap:** While OPEC production was a factor, the workbook specifically attributes the negative prices to pandemic lockdowns. The other options are fictional. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is unique to commodity markets — financial derivatives (equity, currency) cannot have negative prices in normal circumstances. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This event demonstrates the unique risk of physically-settled commodity futures — storage costs matter. Clients in commodity derivatives must understand delivery obligations.
